So what if marketing wasn't a process of pushing your product down your customer's throat, but actually a process of helping them engineer a new reality where your product made perfect sense to them? And what if your sales messaging could be more collaborative, contextually relevant, and see not just as valuable, but actually a gift to your audience? And what if marketing and copyrighting truly could be a win-win for your audience? And finally, what if doing so wasn't at the job of the copywriter, but the job of the coach? A lot of these questions, but a goldmine of rewards for the marketer brave enough to dive down that rabbit hole and explore. And on that note, I want to invite you to Module 1. So quick disclaimer before we really get started, but this is going to be the most concept heavy module of the course. But I'm going to ask you not to skip it, making so many conventional ideas and just flipping them on their head. So this is about giving you a whole new paradigm of which to see and execute your marketing and copyrighting. And these concepts really will set the foundation for everything you're going to learn and apply throughout 10x launches. So even if you got a quick win from a theme plate or something else, maybe you got Tomorrow Y by jumping ahead into Module 7, I'm going to ask that you not get too addicted to the rush of those wins. Because by all means, get them, but come back, steep yourself in these principles, and I promise you it will forever change the way you approach this little thing we call copyrighting. So with all that said, here is what you are going to discover inside Module 1. So you are going to get your first deep dive introduction of the coaching, the conversion method. You're going to learn the four foundations of which this whole method relies on. And then we're going to dig a little deeper into the art of sequencing, what I call engineering the conversion context. How do you take multiple emails across multiple phases of your launch and make sure that they all sync up and play their own part in engineering the conversion. And then we're going to take a look at what those five core subsequences are. So this is for every launch and funnel. As we said in the welcome, this really is funnel agnostic. Most, if not every launch method will follow the same kind of rhythm. The way they get presented and the modalities of them and the mediums may be different, but it's really these five core subsequences that are at play no matter what funnel you are using. And then we're going to dig into a concept I call direct response 2.0. This is a term that's been around for literally over 150 years. I mean, everyone claims that it is the holy grail of copywriting and marketing. And that is true to a certain extent, but as our market sophistication evolves, as our tools evolve, so too must our definition of what we're doing and how we see ourselves within that marketing scape, if you will. So we're going to explore a new definition around direct response. And then we're going to wrap up really with what I call the five Cs of a successful conversion coach. So these are really the traits and characteristics that you're going to start embodying. So as I said earlier, copywriting is not a process of memorizing more formulas or tricks or persuasion hacks or anything like that, but really it's a lot more foundational and really in your approach. So these five Cs of a successful conversion coach are really going to give you a new way of thinking and a new way of interacting with your audience that are really going to pay dividends throughout your funnel and really draw them in closer, create that intimacy, and lead you down a successful path. So that is what we have in store. That is the overview. I'm going to take a little pause and I will meet you back in the next video.
